text: The Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering (MSAJCE), established on 5th July 2001 under the aegis of Mohamed Sathak Trust, is approved by AICTE New Delhi, affiliated to Anna University Chennai and Government of Tamil Nadu. MSAJCE strives to continuously upgrade its facilities to provide quality technical education to meet the industrial and societal needs, by providing skill-based training with state of the art facilities. The college campus, sprawling over 70 acres of greenery, is located inside the SIPCOT IT Park, Siruseri, and is surrounded by multinational IT companies such as TCS, CTS, Intellect, Aspire, Steria, Polaris, FSS, etc. MSAJCE has good infrastructure facilities such as spacious classrooms, drawing halls, laboratories, seminar halls, computer facilities with internet and wifi connectivity, library, workshops, auditorium and playground. The college is well connected by Road, Rail and Air from various parts of the country. A team of well qualified and experienced faculty supports the teaching and learning process. The college provides equal importance for academic, co-curricular and extracurricular activities. MSAJCE provides all kinds of training for the professional and overall transformation of the students. The vision of MSJACE is to be a premier institution that produces globally competent and socially responsible engineers and entrepreneurs. The mission of MSJACE is to provide quality education through innovative teaching-learning practices, state-of-the-art infrastructure, and a conducive learning environment, to foster research and development, entrepreneurship, and lifelong learning, and to instill ethical values and social responsibility. Mohamed Sathak Trust, a charitable and educational organization, was established on 26 October 1973 at Kilakarai, by the Philanthropic Mohamed Sathak Family of Kilakarai, Ramanathapuram District, Tamil Nadu, with the sole purpose of helping deprived people from rural and financially challenged backgrounds to obtain quality education. The first self-financing engineering college in the country was established by the Trust in 1984 at Kilakarai. From that day onwards, the Trust has been contributing to provide quality education with academic excellence to meet the requirements of industries at national and international levels. The Trust has an impeccable track record of running quality educational institutions from higher secondary level to undergraduate and postgraduate levels in various disciplines such as Engineering Technology, Arts and Science, Pharmacy, Physiotherapy, Nursing and Medical Sciences. It has 18 educational institutions at present in Kilakarai, Ramanathapuram and Chennai. The Trust's guiding philosophy: "God will not change the condition of a people until they try to change themselves." The Trust focuses on doing right things without any bias and with a charitable mind to build institutions of academic excellence and help in building the nation. section: 1. Department Overview — Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS)
page_title: 1. Department Overview — Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS)
text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the process of creating machines to think like humans. AI is a term used to describe the simulation of human intelligence processes in machines. It typically involves sophisticated algorithms that enable computers to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition and decision making. Data Science is a field of knowledge which applies advanced statistical and mathematical techniques to large datasets in order to extract knowledge from them. Data can be structured like a spreadsheet or unstructured like text or video data. Data science is the application of techniques from statistics, mathematics, computer programming and other fields to solve real-world problems using information technology. Due to the exploration of the Internet, AI and Data Science deliver data-driven solutions using computational principles, methods, and systems for extracting information from data and modern computational systems that reveal capabilities of insight, way of thinking, learning, and action that are typical of human intelligence. B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) has emerged as one of the most high-growth, dynamic, and lucrative careers in technology since 2015. This course aims at providing core technologies such as artificial intelligence, data mining, and data modeling, and also gives intensive inputs in areas of machine learning and big data analytics. Students gain cross-disciplinary skills across fields such as statistics, computer science, machine learning, and logic. Career opportunities exist in healthcare, business, e-commerce, social networking companies, climatology, biotechnology, genetics, and other important areas. The major focus of this programme is to equip students with statistical, mathematical reasoning, machine learning, knowledge discovery, and visualization skills. The programme also deals with core principles and ethical issues in Data Science and AI, a

Scientist, Technology Developer, IT Specialist, Technical Consultant, Quality Analyst, Information Network Manager, and Data Quality Analyst across Manufacturing, Software, Research, Education, Defense, Banking & Finance, e-Commerce, Transport and Health Science. This chunk answers what is B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science at MSAJCE, what is the AIDS programme about at MSAJCE, and what is Artificial Intelligence in the context of MSAJCE AIDS department. It also covers what is Data Science at MSAJCE, what career opportunities are available after AIDS from MSAJCE, and what industries can AIDS graduates from MSAJCE work in. Further topics include what is the focus of the B.Tech AIDS programme at MSAJCE, what real-world AI applications are relevant to the AIDS course at MSAJCE, and what skills will students gain from the AIDS programme at MSAJCE, as well as whether AIDS is a good programme in terms of career growth.

section: 2. B.Tech AIDS Programme Details, Vision, Mission and OBE (PEOs, PSOs, POs)

text: AIDS course at MSAJCE, and what skills will students gain from the AIDS programme at MSAJCE, as well as whether AIDS is a good programme in terms of career growth. The B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) program is a 4-year regular program or a 3-year lateral entry program. The program consists of 8 semesters for regular entry and 6 semesters for lateral entry. The total intake is 30 seats, with 15 government seats and 15 management seats. Eligibility requires a 10+2 system of education with a pass in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics. The curriculum and syllabus follow the 2021 Regulation. The scope for higher studies includes M.E., M.Tech., and M.B.A. The vision is to be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Artificial Intelligence and Data Science users through sustainable practices. The mission includes imparting core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science through innovative teaching and learning methodology. It also includes inculcating critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning, and creativity needed for industry and society, and cultivating students with all-round competencies for career, higher education, and self-employability. The program educational objectives include preparing graduates for analysing, designing, developing, and testing software solutions and products with creativity and sustainability. Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements, and they will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms. The program specific outcomes include developing creative solutions by adapting emerging technologies / tools for real-time applications of Industry and applying the acquired knowledge to develop software solutions and innovative mobile apps for various automation applications. The programme outcomes include applying the knowled

these to one's own work, and recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change. The Artificial Intelligence and Data Science sectors in India are expected to have explosive growth over the next five years. AI-driven solutions and data analytics are driving better decisions across all sectors, and the demand for professionals in these fields is expected to grow significantly. Increased AI Adoption Across Industries includes Healthcare, Finance, E-commerce, Telecommunications, Automotive, and Manufacturing. AI will be used for Predictive analytics, Personalized services, Data-driven decision-making, and Automation. AI-driven solutions will be used in core business functions such as HR, finance, and customer service. Government Support and Initiatives such as Digital India, National AI Strategy, and the National AI Mission initiatives boost AI research, startups, and adoption across public services. India aims to be a global leader in AI for Smart cities, Healthcare, and Agriculture. There is a rising demand for Data-Driven Decision Making including Customer segmentation, Fraud detection, and Supply chain optimization. There is growth in AI-Powered Startups as AI startups are expected to double in the next few years, creating job opportunities for AI engineers, data scientists, and product managers. There is Integration of AI into Business Functions as Finance, retail, e-commerce, and manufacturing are increasingly adopting AI-based automation to streamline operations, reduce costs, and offer personalized experiences. Data Scientists analyze large datasets to derive actionable insights, build predictive models, and help organizations make data-driven decisions, with skills in Python, R, SQL, Machine Learning Algorithms, Statistical analysis, Data visualization using Tableau and Power BI, and Deep learning, and are in high demand across healthcare, finance, retail, and e-comme

Data Engineers design, construct, and maintain large-scale data pipelines, working with big data technologies to ensure data is stored, processed, and accessible, with skills in SQL, Python, Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, AWS, Google Cloud, and ETL processes, and are in rising demand with the growth of big data and cloud computing in data-driven companies. PO 11 is Project Management, which means to demonstrate knowledge of engineering and management principles as member and leader. PO 12 is Life-long Learning, which means to recognize need for and engage in independent and life-long learning in technological change.

The following data is from the Driver, Key Technologies / Areas, Job Impact table: Digital Transformation uses SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics, SaaS, and ERP and has an impact on ERP Consultants and Business Systems Analysts. AI, ML and Analytics uses Python, Hadoop, Spark, Tableau, and Power BI and has an impact on Data Scientists, BI Analysts, and ML Engineers. Cloud Computing uses AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Serverless, and multi-cloud and has an impact on Cloud Architects and Cloud Engineers. E-Commerce Growth uses AI chatbots, recommendation systems, and supply chain and has an impact on Data Scientists and Systems Analysts. FinTech Expansion uses Blockchain, digital payments, and AI financial services and has an impact on FinTech Professionals and Blockchain Developers. Healthcare Digitalization uses Digital records, telemedicine, and AI diagnostics and has an impact on Healthcare IT Professionals. Smart Manufacturing (Industry 4.0) uses IoT, AI, and cloud in manufacturing and has an impact on IT-Business Bridge Professionals. Cyber Security & Compliance uses Firewalls, encryption, GDPR, and Data Protection Bill and has an impact on Cyber Security Specialists and Compliance Experts.

The IQAC or Internal Quality Assurance Cell is a quality enhancement and sustenance system of MSAJCE. It is a facilitative and participative management system. The IQAC is a consistent driving force for planning, guiding and monitoring Quality Assurance and Quality Enhancement activities of the college. It was established on 20-08-2012 as per the NAAC guidelines. The IQAC assures the stakeholders i.e., students, parents, teachers, staff, management, funding agencies and society in general of the accountability and transparency in the quality education concern in the institution. It also coordinates various activities like application for accreditation, preparation of AQAR, organization of workshops and seminars on quality related themes, and promote quality circles.

education concern in the institution. It also coordinates various activities like application for accreditation, preparation of AQAR, organization of workshops and seminars on quality related themes, and promote quality circles. In pursuance of the National Action Plan of the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC), Bangalore, for performance evaluation, assessment and accreditation and quality upgradation of institutions of higher education, the NAAC proposes that every accredited institution should establish an Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) as a post-accreditation quality sustenance measure. Since quality enhancement is a continuous process, the IQAC will become a part of an institution's system and work towards realizing the goals of quality enhancement and sustenance. The prime task of the IQAC is to develop a system for conscious, consistent and catalytic improvement in the performance of institutions. The IQAC will make a significant and meaningful contribution in the post-accreditation phase of institutions. During the post-accreditation period, the IQAC will channelise the efforts and measures of an institution towards academic excellence. The guidelines provided will facilitate the institution in the creation and operation of the IQAC. The work of the IQAC is the first step towards the internalization and institutionalization of quality enhancement. Its success depends upon the sense of belongingness and participation it can inculcate in all the constituents of the institution. It will not be yet another hierarchical structure or record-keeping exercise in the institution; it will be a facilitative and participative voluntary system of the institution. The IQAC has the potential to become a vehicle for ushering in quality by working out intervention strategies to remove deficiencies and enhance quality. Quality circles in industries operate on similar lines. IQAC Page URL: <https://www.msjace-edu.in/iqac.php> NAAC Page: <https://www.ms

for ushering in quality by working out intervention strategies to remove deficiencies and enhance quality. Quality circles in industries operate on similar lines. IQAC Page URL: <https://www.msjace-edu.in/iqac.php> NAAC Page: <https://www.msjace-edu.in/naac.php> The following data is from the Objectives of IQAC table: Developing a quality system for conscious, consistent and catalytic programmed action to improve the academic and administrative performance of the institution is an objective of IQAC. Promoting measures for institutional functioning towards quality enhancement through internalization of quality culture and institutionalization of best practices is an objective of IQAC. The following data is from the Strategies of IQAC table: Ensuring timely, efficient and progressive performance of academic, administrative and financial tasks is a strategy of IQAC. Relevant and quality academic and research programmes are part of the strategies of IQAC. Equitable access to and affordability of academic programmes for various sections of the society is a strategy of IQAC. Optimization and integration of modern methods of teaching and learning is a strategy of IQAC. The credibility of the evaluation process is a strategy of IQAC. Ensuring the adequacy, maintenance and proper allocation of support structure and services is a strategy of IQAC. Sharing of research findings and networking with other institutions in India and abroad is a strategy of IQAC. The following data is from the Functions of IQAC table: Development and application of quality benchmarks or parameters for various academic and administrative activities of the institution is a function of IQAC. Facilitating the creation of a learner centric environment conducive to quality education and faculty maturation to adopt the required knowledge and technology for participatory teaching and learning process is a function of IQAC. Arrangement for feedback responses from students, parents and other stakeholders on q

Quality Culture in the institution is a function of IQAC. Preparation of the Annual Quality Assurance Report (AQAR) as per guidelines and recommendations of NAAC, is a function of IQAC. The role of the coordinator of the IQAC is crucial in ensuring the effective functioning of all the members. The coordinator of the IQAC may be a senior person with expertise in quality aspects. She/he may be a full-time functionary or, to start with, she/he may be a senior academic/administrator entrusted with the IQAC as an additional responsibility. Secretarial assistance may be facilitated by the administration. It is preferable that the coordinator may have a sound knowledge about the computer, its various functions and usage for effective communication.

Secretarial assistance may be facilitated by the administration. It is preferable that the coordinator may have a sound knowledge about the computer, its various functions and usage for effective communication. Quality assurance is a by-product of ongoing efforts to define the objectives of an institution, to have a work plan to achieve them and to specify the checks and balances to evaluate the degree to which each of the tasks is fulfilled. Hence devotion and commitment to improvement rather than mere institutional control is the basis for devising procedures and instruments for assuring quality. The right balance between the health and growth of an institution needs to be struck. The IQAC has to ensure that whatever is done in the institution for education is done efficiently and effectively with high standards. In order to do this, the IQAC will have to first establish procedures and modalities to collect data and information on various aspects of institutional functioning. The coordinators of the IQAC and the secretary have a major role in implementing these functions.

PO2 Problem Analysis means to identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. Job opportunities are available in various MNCs in service and product companies as System Engineers, Software Professionals, Network Engineers, System Analysts, System Administrators, Project Leaders, Education sector, System Architects and Academicians. The Information Technology (IT) industry in India is poised for strong growth over the next five years, driven by domestic and global trends. Businesses across all sectors are adopting Cloud computing, AI, Big Data, IoT, and Blockchain, so demand for cloud engineers, data scientists, AI specialists, and DevOps engineers will rise. The Cloud market in India is growing rapidly, and AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud will create high demand for cloud architects, cloud administrators, and cloud security specialists, and hybrid cloud solutions will generate opportunities in cloud migration, cloud operations, and cloud infrastructure management. AI is transforming healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and retail, and by 2030, demand will rise for data scientists, AI researchers, machine learning engineers, and AI-enabled software developers, requiring skills such as AI ethics, deep learning, and AI model deployment. With increasing cyberattacks and regulations (GDPR, Personal Data Protection Bill), demand for cybersecurity experts will grow, creating roles for ethical hackers, incident response professionals, security architects, and SOC analysts. India's cashless economy push requires blockchain developers, smart contract auditors, and DeFi specialists. The rollout of 5G will create demand for 5G network engineers, telecommunications architects, and telecom software developers, and IoT will see a boom in smart cities, agriculture, and healthcare. Government initiatives like Digital India, Smart Cities Mission, and Make in India will create roles for IT

section: --- 5. IT Domain Employment Opportunities and Salary Expectations
page_title: --- 5. IT Domain Employment Opportunities and Salary Expectations
text: This section covers 5. IT Domain Employment Opportunities and Salary Expectations in the IT department at MSAJCE. It is divided into 2 sub-sections covering: Part 1; Part 2.

section: --- 5. IT Domain Employment Opportunities and Salary Expectations — Part 1
page_title: --- 5. IT Domain Employment Opportunities and Salary Expectations — Part 1
text: managers, data engineers, IoT developers, and cloud solutions architects. India will continue as a global IT outsourcing hub with demand for application developers, IT consultants, systems integrators, and support specialists. The following data is from the Employment Opportunities in IT Domain (2025–2030) section: Job roles in cloud computing and virtualization include Cloud Engineer, Cloud Solutions Architect, Cloud Security Specialist, DevOps Engineer, and Cloud Administrator. Required skills include AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Cloud security (IAM, encryption), Containers (Docker, Kubernetes), and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible). Relevant certifications are AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Microsoft Certified Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Google Cloud Professional Cloud Architect, and Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA). Job roles in artificial intelligence and machine learning include Data Scientist, AI Engineer, Machine Learning Engineer, NLP Specialist, and Deep Learning Engineer. Skills required are Python, R, Java; ML frameworks (TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch); Data visualization (Matplotlib, Seaborn); and knowledge of AI/ML algorithms and statistical models. Certifications include Microsoft Certified Azure AI Engineer Associate, Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer, IBM AI Engineering Professional Certificate, and AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty. Cyber Security job roles include Security Analyst, Ethical Hacker (Penetration Tester), Security Architect, SOC Analyst, and Cybersecurity Consultant. section: 1. Placement Cell Overview — Mission, Objectives & Placement Committee
page_title: 1. Placement Cell Overview — Mission, Objectives & Placement Committee
text: The chunk describes the overall mission and objectives of the MSAJCE Placement Cell and lists the full placement committee with names and positions. In a competitive job market, employers expect graduates to have a range of skills and right experience of the workplace. MSAJCE provides at most care, support and ample opportunities to help students develop requisite skills and expertise and make them employable graduates. The Placement Cell Objectives are to provide Employment Opportunities for students in reputed companies, to organize On and Off Campus Recruitment Drives for students, to Train students on employability skills such as Aptitude Skills, Communication Skills and Technical Skills, to conduct Value Added Certification courses to gain domain specific knowledge, to conduct Mock Aptitude, Mock Interview, Mock GD to prepare students to face the interview with confidence, to arrange In-plant training, Internship and Industrial visits, and to organize workshops on Recent Technologies by industrial experts which benefits students to understand technological innovations. Besides providing support to entrepreneurial ventures, MSAJCE keeps its employability services up to date with the demands of the job market and aims to ensure that graduates are in the strongest position possible when they leave. This chunk describes the physical and infrastructure facilities available at MSAJCE for conducting placement activities including written tests, group discussions, interviews and career counselling. MSAJCE has well-furnished air-conditioned rooms for written tests, group discussions, and face-to-face interviews, well-established computer labs for online training and examinations, air-conditioned auditorium, seminar halls and meeting halls, and dedicated AC rooms for conducting group discussions. Career counselling is available on campus directing students to choose the best possible stream that meets their requirement in a unique way, and the students have mentors who handle their problems and make them focused about their targets. In pursuit of MSAJCE's vision and mission to make students deployable and equip them with upcoming emerging industrial demands, various Technology Centres were established in the year 2019-2020 to educate, train and up-skill students in niche technologies. The courses and training are designed based on the recommendations of AICTE and India Skill Report 2020. The Technology Centers, established with state-of-art facilities, provide an ambient learning environment for research and innovation. Each centre is collaborated with industry/government organizations to provide certifications, onsite/offsite projects and internships.

The Technology Centers, established with state-of-art facilities, provide an ambient learning environment for research and innovation. Each centre is collaborated with industry/government organizations to provide certifications, onsite/offsite projects and internships. 1. 3D Printing (Additive Manufacturing) — 6 Hours: Introduction, Process, Classifications, Advantages, Additive vs Conventional Manufacturing, Applications. 2. CAD for Additive Manufacturing — 4 Hours: CAD Data formats, Data translation, Data loss, STL format. 3. Additive Manufacturing Techniques — 10 Hours: Stereo-Lithography, LOM, FDM, SLS, SLM, Binder Jet technology. Process parameters, Process Selection. Application Domains: Aerospace, Electronics, Healthcare, Defence, Automotive, Construction, Food Processing, Machine Tools. 4. Materials — 4 Hours: Polymers, Metals, Non-Metals, Ceramics. Various forms of raw material (Liquid, Solid, Wire, Powder). Powder Preparation and their desired properties. Support Materials. 5. Additive Manufacturing Equipment — 4 Hours: Process Equipment Design and parameters, Governing Bonding Mechanism, Common faults and troubleshooting, Process Design. 6. Post Processing — 1 Hour: Support Removal, Sanding, Acetone treatment, Polishing. 7. Product Quality — 1 Hour: Inspection and testing, Defects and their causes.

Introduction to IoT (8 Hours): Architectural Overview – Design principles – IoT Applications – Sensing – Actuation – Basics of Networking – M2M and IoT Technology Fundamentals – Devices and gateways – Data management – Business processes in IoT – Everything as a Service (XaaS) – Role of Cloud in IoT – Security aspects in IoT. 2. Elements of IoT (9 Hours): Hardware Components (Arduino, Raspberry Pi, Communication, Sensing, Actuation, I/O Interfaces) – Software Components – Programming APIs using Python/Node.js/Arduino – Communication Protocols: MQTT, ZigBee, Bluetooth, CoAP, UDP, TCP. 3. IoT Application Development (18 Hours): Solution framework for IoT applications – Implementation of Device integration – Data acquisition and integration – Device data storage (unstructured data on cloud/local server) – Authentication and authorization of devices. 4. IoT Case Studies (10 Hours): Mini projects in Industrial automation, Transportation, Agriculture, Healthcare, Home Automation.

Students will be able to learn technical and design aspects of AR and VR, create 3D design, Visualization and Animation for various objects and scenes, apply virtual and augmented reality methods, tools and equipment in industry, education and social life, and gain an overview of the concepts and working of AR-VR techniques. Students will be able to build basic applications considering technical and design aspects of AR and VR, develop PC/Mobile-based VR applications, develop 3D Visualization and Visualization projects, and design applications in Gaming, Architecture, Manufacturing, Retail, Healthcare, etc.

applications considering technical and design aspects of AR and VR, develop PC/Mobile-based VR applications, develop 3D Visualization and Visualization projects, and design applications in Gaming, Architecture, Manufacturing, Retail, Healthcare, etc. 1. Introduction to Virtual Reality (6 Hours): Fundamental Concept and Components of VR – Primary Features and Present Development – Computer graphics – Real time computer graphics – Flight Simulation – Benefits of VR – Historical development – 3D Computer Graphics: Virtual world space, positioning the virtual observer, perspective projection, human vision, stereo perspective projection, 3D clipping, Colour theory, Simple 3D modelling, Illumination models, Reflection models, Shading algorithms, Radiosity, Hidden Surface Removal, Realism-Stereographic image. 2. Interactive Techniques in VR (6 Hours): From 2D to 3D – 3D space curves – 3D boundary representation – Geometric Transformations – Frames of reference – Modeling transformations – Instances – Picking – Flying – Scaling the VE – Collision detection – Generic VR system: Virtual environment, Computer environment, VR technology, Model of interaction, VR Systems. 3. Visual Computation in VR (6 Hours): Animating the Virtual Environment – Dynamics of numbers – Linear and Nonlinear interpolation – Animation of objects – Shape and object inbetweening – Free-form deformation – Particle system – Physical Simulation: Objects in gravitational field, Rotating wheels, Elastic collisions, Projectiles, Simple pendulum, Springs, Flight dynamics. 4. Augmented and Mixed Reality (6 Hours): Taxonomy, technology and features of AR – Difference between AR and VR – Challenges with AR – AR systems and functionality – AR methods – Visualization techniques – Wireless displays in educational AR – Mobile projection interfaces – Marker-less tracking – Enhancing interactivity in AR – Evaluating AR systems. 5. Multiple Models of Input and Output Interface in VR (6 Hours): Human factors – VR Hardwa

Devices. 6. Application of VR in Digital Entertainment (6 Hours): VR in Film & TV Production – VR in Physical Exercises and Games – Demonstration of Digital Entertainment by VR. The objectives are to gain basic theoretical knowledge and practical skills of BIM techniques, interpret 2D & 3D drawings and structural analysis, understand regulations as per National Building Code, and prepare students for actual work situations through practical training. The outcomes are to prepare a component to meet desired needs within realistic constraints (economic, environmental, social, ethical, sustainability), create 2D drawings of building, industrial structures and various framed structures using AUTOCAD software, create and simulate a virtual building model using REVIT ARCHITECTURE software with intelligent building elements, and design and analyse different types of structures (liquid retaining structures, bridge components, retaining wall, industrial and commercial structures) in 3D form using STAAD.Pro software to predict performances before construction. Software Used: AUTOCAD, REVIT ARCHITECTURE, STAAD.Pro

Students will be able to become expert bilinguals to learn work ethic and business etiquette in career opportunities, gain deeper knowledge and higher understanding of foreign languages, boost professional value in Business, Social Science, Humanities, Technology and Science, get exposure to culture ingrained in the people of various countries, improve higher thinking skills, new learning strategies, and exposure to advanced technologies, and grow as leaders in many leading sectors to drive for innovation. The focus is on Language Skills (LSRW — Listening, Speaking, Reading, Writing), Foreign Language Proficiency, International Employability, and Business Communication.

MIT Square is an ISO Certified Company headquartered in Bangalore (Silicon Valley of India) and registered office in London (Silicon Valley of UK). It is a registered MSME recognised by the Ministry of MSME, Govt. of India and recognised by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs, Govt. of UK. Network offices in USA, Australia and Singapore. MIT stands for 'Management and Innovation for Transformation'. Square represents 'People + Process + Product + Profit' — tagline: 'We transform your life'. Core technical team from IITs and Universities in London. MIT Square is a globally trusted brand in EduTech, HealthTech, AgroTech and GreenTech supporting: incubation/start-up, business operations, talent enablement (training & learning), talent acquisition, innovations, patent filing, product design & development, manufacturing and supply of technological and non-technological products.

HealthTech, AgroTech and GreenTech supporting: incubation/start-up, business operations, talent enablement (training & learning), talent acquisition, innovations, patent filing, product design & development, manufacturing and supply of technological and non-technological products. The objectives are to create innovative applications and domain capability for Smart City, Smart Health, Smart Manufacturing, and Smart Agriculture; to build industry capable talent, a startup community, and an entrepreneurial ecosystem; to provide an ecosystem for innovation and entrepreneurship; to energize a research mind-set and reduce costs in R&D with neutral, interoperable, multi-technology stack laboratory facilities; to reduce import dependency on technology components and promote indigenization; to position the institution as a provider of end-to-end solutions in education and technology; to provide training and learning, including internships, both online and offline; to provide an environment for product creation, testing, validation, and incubation; and to provide learning in breakthrough technologies, specifically AR/VR, Data Analytics, and AI.

The RAISE CoE provides a great learning and leadership opportunity, allowing students to become coding gurus. It aims to spread Techno-Entrepreneurship Culture within the Campus through events, workshops and hackathons, while also providing Internship Opportunities. The CoE offers the opportunity to work in startups or prestigious companies, with the possibility to study abroad. Students also have the opportunity to intern or work full-time abroad, and can receive a Personalised LinkedIn recommendation, along with a Letter of Recommendation after successful Internship/techno programs completion.

The department has a fully Techno-Entrepreneurship Innovation lab with hardware and software tools with licenses for development and research projects. The department also has recognition as one of the top National Academies for the innovation lab. They provide essential curriculum and resource kits for comprehensive training and industry readiness, alongside scalable infrastructure to extend training to corporates and professionals from other industries. The department provides up to 10 years assured training support from MIT Square to empower faculty, research scholars and students and, based on NEP 2020, support is provided in regional languages as well. The government initiatives supported are Digital India, Make in India, Startup India, Standup India, Skill India and Self-reliant India (Atmanirbhar Bharat). Alumni Association Overview and Objectives Chunk ID: msajce_alumni_chunk_01 URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/alumni.php#tab1 ~370 tokens Context: Describes the purpose, mission, and seven core objectives of the MSAJCE Alumni Association, useful for students and alumni wanting to know what the association does. MSAJCE Alumni Association Overview Alumni association of Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering welcomes you all to join us. This association is formed to reconnect and foster intellectual and emotional relationship between alumni and parent organisation. Also to bring in goodwill and voluntary commitment to support future engineers of the institution and provide industry-institute relationship and enthusiastically participate in various activities organised by the college. Our Objectives: 1. To foster a sense of connectedness back to the institution 2. To establish and renew student relationship 3. To involve in various activities of the college 4. To provide assistance to needful students by way of funds and scholarship 5. To gather and maintain database of employment and assist in placements 6. To arrange teaching and training classes to students 7. MSAJCE Energy and Eco Club Energy Club: The goal of the Energy Club is to raise and spread knowledge about responsible and sustainable energy use. Given the limited quantity of fossil fuels available, resource management and conservation are urgently needed. Students are introduced to the concept of energy conservation through the Energy Club, as well as the necessity for it and what part they can play in guaranteeing conservation. In order to combat the ongoing energy issue, it helps the students to conserve energy. These actions would support not only the dissemination of information about energy efficiency but also its application in homes and institutions. Eco Club: The Eco Club holds that nature imparts life lessons in its own steady ways and empowers our young students to share their responsibility for upholding environmental health and taking action to prevent environmental degradation both individually and collectively. B.Tech in Computer Science and Business Systems (CSBS) is a specialized undergraduate engineering program designed to blend core computer science education with insights into business systems and management. Introduced in collaboration with Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) to bridge the gap between traditional computer science education and the dynamic needs of the IT and business world in the era of Business 4.0. Curriculum covers: Analytics, Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, Business Analytics, Financial Management, Economics, Internet of Things, Design Thinking, humanities and value-added science. Key Features: (cid:127) Balanced Curriculum Core CS (Data Structures, Algorithms, OS, DBMS, AI/ML) + Business subjects (Economics, Financial Management, Business Process Management). (cid:127) Industry Collaboration Designed in partnership with TCS; emphasis on employability and industry readiness. (cid:127) Focus Areas Problem-solving using computational thinking, Business analytics, IT systems for business applications. (cid:127) Soft Skills Training Communication, Teamwork, Entrepreneurial thinking. Benefits Over Regular CSE: More interdisciplinary exposure; better suited for tech-enabled business functions; greater alignment with industry expectations in IT services and consulting. Department of Computer Science and Engineering MSAJCE Computer Science and Engineering is one of the popular courses among engineering students. It mainly focuses on programming, computation, analysis of algorithm, program design and testing, software engineering etc for design, development and analysis of software and hardware used to solve the problem of various businesses, scientific and social issues. CSE integrates both computer hardware and software and is intended to develop solid understanding of the entire computer system and its accessories and software. It provides sophistication to the world and touches human lives in all aspects such as education, transportation, entertainment, banking, social media, healthcare and supply chain management. CSE is an interdisciplinary subject linked with psychology, geology science, maths, art, music, bio informatics, electronics, and operation research. Modern Tool Usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations. PO7. Environment and Sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. PO8. Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice. PO9. Individual and Team Work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multi-disciplinary settings. PO10. Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions. PO11. Project Management and Finance: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects and in multi-disciplinary environments. PO12. Life-long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change. Rise in job demand in PCB design, VLSI, microelectronics, and hardware design. 5. Digital India and 5G Rollout: Massive growth in Telecom infrastructure, Optical fiber networks, and IoT ecosystems. National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) MSAJCE Vision: To cater the needs of student entrepreneurs with innovative ideas thereby introducing a culture of entrepreneurship inside campus which will strengthen our education system and thereby promoting national economic and social growth. Mission: To develop an ecosystem with required infrastructure that can enable students and faculty to innovate and prototype their potential ideas with industrial standards and support from Government, industry and reputed academic institutions around the world and help them to realize their potentials. Short Term Objectives: 1. To help student groups to prototype their ideas. 2. To improve innovation, creative and design thinking among student community. 3. Incubation facility for faculty driven start-up and student/alumni start-up. 4. Organize FDP, seminars and workshops, distinguish talks for students, faculty and alumni and promote entrepreneurial culture. 5. Strengthen institute-industry interaction cell activity and effectively use the outcomes for achieving the mission. Long Term Objectives: 1. Associate with DST, CII, MSME and other academic institutions for transferring world class facility for MSAJCE IEDC activities. 2. Department Overview and B.Tech IT Programme Details Chunk ID: msajce_it_chunk_01 URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/it.php#tab1 ~480 tokens Overview of the Information Technology department at MSAJCE and full details of the B.Tech IT programme including duration, seats, eligibility, and curriculum links. Department of Information Technology MSAJCE The role of Information Technology is vital in every aspect of human life. The information technology is the study and use of systems for storing, retrieving and processing information. With the introduction of computers, the business world has changed forever; using computers ensure smooth running of the business. Information technology helps the business world to view changes in the global markets faster than they usually do. Due to the development of this technology, globalization has increased and the world is brought closer. Information is stored and shared quickly and easily across the globe. Communication has become easier and cheaper. Using internet, Skype, people can speak to each other all over the globe. Social media and mobile devices make the people closer to each other. The key fields include Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Cloud Computing, Networking, Mobile computing, Block Chain and so on, which have tremendous job openings. Programme: B.Tech. Information Technology Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry) No. of Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry) Intake / No. of Seats: Total 60 (Government 30, Management 30) Eligibility: 10+2 system of Education. Must have secured a pass in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics in the qualifying examination. Curriculum & Syllabus Link

Vision: To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient IT professionals through sustainable practices. Mission: M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Information Technology through innovative teaching and learning methodology M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs): PEO1 PEO Description PEO1 Graduates will be prepared for designing and developing efficient Information Technology systems and software with creativity and sustainability PEO2 Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements PEO3 Graduates will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs): Engineeri

Library Overview, Physical Details and Stack Collection Statistics Chunk ID: msajce_library_chunk_01 URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/library.php ~490 tokens Introduction to the MSAJCE Learning Centre (Library) covering area, technology, collection size, and complete stack collection statistics. Welcome to the Learning Centre MSAJCE Library is the Heart of every academic Institution. Our institution encourages all students and staff members to make the best use of the library that has been carefully built up since the inception of the college as a cardinal support service of the Institute. The prime objective of our library is to provide the right information to the right readers at the right time in the right manner by bibliographic sources and full text sources in the form of print as well as digital in the disciplines of Science, Engineering, Technology, Humanities, Management, General and etc. The collections are kept in separate places such as the stack section, reference section, and Periodical Section to support the scholarly and informational needs of the Institute Community. The library is housed in a spacious area of 8978 Sq.ft (Ground Floor and 1st Floor), fully computerized with more than 29853 books and 1885 reference volumes stacked for the benefit of staff and students. The library subscribes around 35 nos specialized Printed journals and 20 nos magazines. We are also a member of DELNET. J-Gate database is subscribed yearly. Technology: Bar-coded Technology, open source software called Koha, internet connectivity and WIFI facility are all available. Industry Institution Interaction (III) Cell Objectives, MoUs and Industries Visited Chunk ID: msajce_placement_chunk_08 URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/placement.php#tab6 ~550 tokens n This chunk covers the III Cell objectives, complete list of 43 MoUs signed with industries from 2017 to 2023, and a selected list of industries visited by MSAJCE students. MSAJCE Industry Institution Interaction (III) Cell: Technical institutes must bridge the gap between industry and academia. In a competitive market due to increasing globalization of business, MSAJCE III Cell collaborates with industries to fulfil mutual needs. MSAJCE-III Cell helps students and faculty understand present industrial expectations and relevant skill sets, identifies skill gaps and provides upgrade opportunities, and enables faculty to train students to meet industrial needs. III Cell Objectives: (1) Sign MoUs with industries for mutual benefit. (2) Organize workshops, conferences, seminars, guest lectures and symposia. (3) Encourage faculty to undergo industry training. (4) Engage industrial experts to interact with students. (5) Involve industrial experts in value-added course development. (6) Arrange industrial visits, internships and project

Higher Education Cell Functions: (1) Create awareness among students on higher education in India and abroad. (2) Motivate students to reach their goal. CSI Computer Society of India Vision: "IT for Masses" Keeping in mind the interest of IT professionals and computer users, CSI works towards making the profession an area of choice amongst all sections of society. CSI regularly organizes conferences, conventions, lectures, projects, and awards. It also provides training for physically challenged citizens ('Punarjani') and assists governments in formulating IT strategy and planning. Region VII Covers: Tamil Nadu, Pondicherry, Andaman and Nicobar, Kerala, Lakshadweep. Kanchipuram Chapter The Society has developed a well-established network across the country. Activities for students include lecture meetings, seminars, conferences, training programs, programming contests, and industrial visits. CSI's Educational Directorate undertakes certification of professionals on the latest technologies and offers distance education in Business Domain areas. ISHRAE Indian Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air Conditioning Engineers ISHRAE was founded in 1981 at New Delhi by a group of eminent HVAC&R; professionals. Ministries/Departments including BIS, BEE (ECBC), MoEFCC (Ozone Cell), and NCCD (Ministry of Agriculture). Department of Science and Humanities (S&H;) MSAJCE The department of S&H; was started in the year 2000. This department lays the base for furthering into one's engineering career. Well experienced faculty with four doctorates contribute much in developing and moulding the students in enhancing their knowledge to comprehend the basic subjects for better exposure. Students are guided through with innovative teaching methods to make learning enjoyable. The laboratories available are well equipped to put into practice what the students have learnt in theory classes. In addition to Literary and Debating Club activity, students are also exposed to various activities like NSS, YRC, Physical Education and Yoga. The department holds the responsibility in making student-teacher and parent-teacher interaction possible. Vision: To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient engineers through sustainable problems. Mission: (cid:127) M1. Impart fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Science & Humanities through innovative teaching and learning methodology (cid:127) M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for Industry and Society (cid:127) M3. MSAJCE Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering Technology Centre Programme & Course Details 1. Technology Centre Overview and Purpose Technology Centre MSAJCE In pursuit of MSAJCE's vision and mission to make students deployable and equip them with upcoming emerging industrial demands, various Technology Centres were established in the year 2019-2020 to educate, train and up-skill students in niche technologies. The courses and training are designed based on the recommendations of AICTE and India Skill Report 2020. The Technology Centers, established with state-of-art facilities, provide an ambient learning environment for research and innovation. Each centre is collaborated with industry/government organizations to provide certifications, onsite/offsite projects and internships. Industry Partners Automation Anywhere, Godrej Inc, Ford, Palo Alto Systems, Amazon Web Services, National Instruments, CISCO Academy, Altair, Open Wave Computing, Levergent Technologies, Propeller Technologies. The AAU powered Bot Lab provides training in RPA, which is simple and powerful automation software used to create software robots that can automate any business process. CISCO Networking Academy Centre Objectives, Outcomes and Certifications CISCO Network Academy Centre About the Centre MSAJCE has partnered with CISCO, the world leaders in networking, to establish the Networking Academy (NetAcad). Cisco Networking Academy is an IT skills and career building program providing unique hands-on learning in networking, security, and cloud technologies using simulation tools. Professional certification is recognized by employers worldwide. The Academy provides career pathways to an estimated 8 million networking jobs worldwide through Netacad. section: Girls Hostel Outing and Security Rules
page_title: Institutional Forensic Facts — Hostel, Scholarships, and Discipline
text: Girls Hostel Outing Rules: Hostellers are generally not permitted to leave the campus except on holidays and require prior written permission from their Head of Department (HOD) and the hostel warden. For outstation travel or going home, written permission from the warden or superintendent is mandatory. Parents must provide a list of authorized visitors or local guardians; outsiders and friends are strictly prohibited from entering rooms. Return times are strictly enforced. Location: Sholinganallur (5 km from campus).

page_title: Institutional Forensic Facts — Hostel, Scholarships, and Discipline
text: Major Events: 'Sathak Thiruvizha' is the annual flagship cultural festival. Technical Symposiums include 'HABIBI', 'Ciphera' (Capture the Flag), and 'SATHAK-A-THON'. Student Council: Comprises President, Secretary, and Department Coordinators. For current office bearer names, students should contact the Head of Student Affairs office.

page_title: Institutional Forensic Facts — Hostel, Scholarships, and Discipline
text: Transport: Public bus route 105 connects Tambaram West to Siruseri IT Park. OMR routes 102, 570, and 221 stop at SIPCOT. The college private fleet covers Chengalpattu, Kanchipuram, and Thiruvallur districts. Transport helpline: +91 94430 10256.

page_title: Institutional Forensic Facts — Hostel, Scholarships, and Discipline
text: MSAJCE Merit Scholarships: Students with a cut-off of 180+ out of 200 are eligible for merit-based tuition waivers. Female students receive a special 10% discount on tuition fees. Government schemes supported include: AICTE Pragati (for girls), AICTE Saksham (for specially-abled), and Merit-cum-means Minority Community scholarships.

page_title: Institutional Forensic Facts — Hostel, Scholarships, and Discipline
text: Anti-Ragging: Headed by the Principal with dedicated squads. Zero tolerance policy—punishments include FIR filing, hostel expulsion, and admission cancellation. Lateral Entry: Admissions conducted via TN-LEA counselling. Candidates must hold a Diploma in Engineering/Technology or a B.Sc. with Mathematics. IMPORTANT: Lateral Entry is strictly for DIRECT SECOND YEAR only. There is NO lateral entry option for the third year.

page_title: Institutional Forensic Facts — Hostel, Scholarships, and Discipline
text: Library Working Hours: The College Main Library is kept open until 7:00 PM on all working days (Monday to Saturday) specifically for the benefit of students staying in the hostel. Sunday hours are from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM.

section: Lab Facilities for AIDS and CSBS Departments
page_title: Institutional Forensic Facts — Hostel, Scholarships, and Discipline
text: Lab Facilities for AIDS and CSBS: The newly established Artificial Intelligence & Data Science (AIDS) and Computer Science and Business Systems (CSBS) departments share state-of-the-art laboratory facilities. These include the C Programming Lab, Internet Programming Lab, Operating Systems Lab, OOAD Lab, Web Technology Lab, Networks Lab, and specialized Artificial Intelligence and Data Exploration labs equipped with high-performance computing resources.

page_title: About Us — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
text: MSAJCE — An Overview The Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering (MSAJCE), established on 5th July 2001 under the aegis of Mohamed Sathak Trust, is approved by AICTE New Delhi, affiliated to Anna University Chennai and Government of Tamil Nadu. MSAJCE strives to continuously upgrade its facilities to provide quality technical education to meet the industrial and societal needs, by providing skill-based training with state of the art facilities. The college campus, sprawling over 70 acres of greenery, is located inside the SIPCOT IT Park, Siruseri, and is surrounded by multinational IT companies such as TCS, CTS, Intellect, Aspire, Steria, Polaris, FSS, etc. MSAJCE has good infrastructure facilities such as spacious classrooms, drawing halls, laboratories, seminar halls, computer facilities with internet and wifi connectivity, library, workshops, auditorium and playground. The college is well connected by Road, Rail and Air from various parts of the country. A team of well qualified and experienced faculty supports the teaching and learning process. The college provides equal importance for academic, co-curricular and extracurricular activities. MSAJCE provides all kinds of training for the professional and overall transformation of the students. Address: 34, Rajiv Gandhi Salai (OMR), Siruseri IT Park, Siruseri, Chennai – 603103 Phone: +91 99400 04500 Email: msajce.office@gmail.com Website: www.msajce-edu.in Online Application Form: https://enrollonline.co.in/Registration/Apply/MSAJCE Online Fee Payment: https://www.feepayr.com/ College Video: https://youtu.be/aNVaQWh1Pp4 Brochure/Prospectus: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/College-Prospectus.pdf

Support for Higher Education and becoming an Entrepreneur. Mission: To impart quality technical education by transforming students into professionally competent technocrats. To nurture the students in all-round sustainable development, for career and self employability. To inculcate critical thinking, professional ethics with civic responsibilities by instilling values. section: Mohamed Sathak Trust — History, Founding and Management
page_title: About Us — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
text: Mohamed Sathak Trust — Management Mohamed Sathak Trust, a charitable and educational organization, was established on 26 October 1973 at Kilakarai, by the Philanthropic Mohamed Sathak Family of Kilakarai, Ramanathapuram District, Tamil Nadu, with the sole purpose of helping deprived people from rural and financially challenged backgrounds to obtain quality education. The first self-financing engineering college in the country was established by the Trust in 1984 at Kilakarai. From that day onwards, the Trust has been contributing to provide quality education with academic excellence to meet the requirements of industries at national and international levels. The Trust has an impeccable track record of running quality educational institutions from higher secondary level to undergraduate and postgraduate levels in various disciplines such as Engineering Technology, Arts and Science, Pharmacy, Physiotherapy, Nursing and Medical Sciences. It has 18 educational institutions at present in Kilakarai, Ramanathapuram and Chennai. The Trust's guiding philosophy: "God will not change the condition of a people until they try to change themselves." The Trust focuses on doing right things without any bias and with a charitable mind to build institutions of academic excellence and help in building the nation. Management page: https://www.msajce-edu.in/ourhistory.php Group of Institutions: https://www.msajce-edu.in/groupofinstitutions.php Functional Committees: https://www.msajce-edu.in/FunctionalCommittees.php Governing Council: https://www.msajce-edu.in/governingcouncil.php Planning and Monitoring Board: https://www.msajce-edu.in/planningmonitoringboard.php Academic Advisory Committee: https://www.msajce-edu.in/academicadvisorycommitte.php

text: Chairman Message Dear Parents and Prospective Students, Welcome to Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering, Chennai. Choosing the right institute to pursue your higher studies is one of the most important decisions that you will ever make. It can be the key for the door of your lifelong opportunity for sustainable growth and service. We want you to base your choice at the right place with relevant and reliable education. The success depends on the effort you invest in your own intellectual and professional development. Invest wisely and be assured that MSAJCE faculty and staff pledge their best efforts in helping you to achieve your educational goals. Our standards are challenging and we are dedicating ourselves in helping you to meet the standards. We will judge our institution's success by how best you succeed in realizing your potential as a student and later in your professional career. I invite you to visit our magnificent campus, to witness our state-of-the-art facilities including the Technology Centres, interact with students and faculty, and convince yourselves on why so many bright students have made MSAJCE their choice of institution. I am confident that you will take pride in joining our college. Principal Message — Dr. K.S. section: Admission through TNEA Counselling & Eligibility for Other State Students
page_title: Admission — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
text: ADMISSION THROUGH TNEA COUNSELLING Students who have completed Plus 2 / Diploma may apply for Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA Counselling) for B.E / B.Tech programmes admission conducted by Directorate of Technical Education (DoTE), Chennai. For More Details Please Visit: TNEA: https://tneaonline.org/ DoTE: http://www.tndte.gov.in/site/ Anna University: https://www.annauniv.edu/ ELIGIBILITY FOR STUDENTS FROM OTHER STATES (Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Kerala and all Northern States) Selection of candidates will be based on the qualifying marks (Q) obtained in Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry at the Plus Two or Intermediate level. The qualifying marks (Q) are calculated as follows: Q = (Mathematics / 2) + ((Physics + Chemistry) / 4) where M, P, and C represent the percentage marks in Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry, respectively. For Admission (Students from Other States): For assistance, students can contact Dr. Vamsi Naga Mohan .A, who can communicate in Telugu, Tamil, Malayalam and Hindi. (LATERAL ENTRY) DEGREE COURSES Qualifying Examination — Diploma Candidates: A pass with prescribed minimum eligibility marks in any one of the Diploma in appropriate branch of Engineering / Technology of the State Board of Technical Education and Training, Tamil Nadu / equivalent prescribed for admission to the Degree Course. Qualifying Examination — B.Sc. Candidates: A pass with prescribed minimum eligibility marks in any recognized Bachelor's Degree in Science of minimum 3 years duration with 10+2+3 years pattern and studied core Mathematics as one of the subjects at degree level. Note: Candidates with Degree other than Bachelor of Science are not eligible. For any clarification with regard to Diploma qualification, write a letter to DOTE, get it clarified and then admit students. section: Department Overview — Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS)
page_title: Artificial Intelligence and Data Science — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
text: Department of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science — MSAJCE Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the process of creating machines to think like humans. AI is a term used to describe the simulation of human intelligence processes in machines. It typically involves sophisticated algorithms that enable computers to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition and decision making. Data Science is a field of knowledge which applies advanced statistical and mathematical techniques to large datasets in order to extract knowledge from them. Data can be structured like a spreadsheet or unstructured like text or video data. Data science is the application of techniques from statistics, mathematics, computer programming and other fields to solve real-world problems using information technology. Due to the exploration of the Internet, AI and Data Science deliver data-driven solutions using computational principles, methods, and systems for extracting information from data and modern computational systems that reveal capabilities of insight, way of thinking, learning, and action that are typical of human intelligence. B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) has emerged as one of the most high-growth, dynamic, and lucrative careers in technology since 2015. This course aims at providing core technologies such as artificial intelligence, data mining, and data modeling, and also gives intensive inputs in areas of machine learning and big data analytics. Students gain cross-disciplinary skills across fields such as statistics, computer science, machine learning, and logic. Career opportunities exist in healthcare, business, e-commerce, social networking companies, climatology, biotechnology, genetics, and other important areas. The major focus of this programme is to equip students with statistical, mathematical reasoning, machine learning, knowledge discovery, and visualization skills. The programme also deals w

section: AI and Data Science Growth in India 2025–2030 — Key Drivers and Job Roles
page_title: Artificial Intelligence and Data Science — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
text: AI and Data Science Growth in India by 2025–2030 The Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science sectors in India are expected to have explosive growth over the next five years. AI-driven solutions and data analytics are driving better decisions across all sectors, and the demand for professionals in these fields is expected to grow significantly. Key Drivers: 1. Increased AI Adoption Across Industries — Healthcare, Finance, E-commerce, Telecommunications, Automotive, and Manufacturing. AI will be used for Predictive analytics, Personalized services, Data-driven decision-making, and Automation. AI-driven solutions in core business functions (HR, finance, customer service). 2. Government Support and Initiatives — Digital India, National AI Strategy, and the National AI Mission initiatives boost AI research, startups, and adoption across public services. India aims to be a global leader in AI for Smart cities, Healthcare, and Agriculture. 3. Rising Demand for Data-Driven Decision Making — Customer segmentation, Fraud detection, and Supply chain optimization. 4. Growth in AI-Powered Startups — AI startups expected to double in the next few years, creating job opportunities for AI engineers, data scientists, and product managers. 5. Integration of AI into Business Functions — Finance, retail, e-commerce, and manufacturing increasingly adopting AI-based automation to streamline operations, reduce costs, and offer personalized experiences. Key AI and Data Science Job Roles in India (2025–2030): 1. Data Scientist Roles: Analyze large datasets to derive actionable insights, build predictive models, help organizations make data-driven decisions. Skills: Python, R, SQL, Machine Learning Algorithms, Statistical analysis, Data visualization (Tableau, Power BI), Deep learning. Demand: High demand across healthcare, finance, retail, and e-commerce. 2. Machine Learning Engineer Roles: Build and deploy machine learning models, implement algorithms, optimize model performance for

section: Industry-Specific AI Job Trends, Expected Salary Growth and Required Skills & Certifications
page_title: Artificial Intelligence and Data Science — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
text: Industry-Specific AI and Data Science Job Trends: 1. Healthcare Sector — Diagnostics, Personalized treatment, Drug discovery, Robot-assisted surgery, Bioinformatics, Healthcare data analysis, and Medical AI applications. 2. Finance and Fintech — Fraud detection, Credit scoring, Automated trading, and Personalized financial services. Employers: Banks, Insurance companies, and Fintech startups. 3. E-Commerce and Retail — Personalization, Dynamic pricing, Inventory optimization, and Recommendation systems. 4. Automotive and Autonomous Vehicles — Autonomous driving, Vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication, Predictive maintenance, Computer vision, Sensor data processing, and AI-powered automotive solutions. 5. Telecommunication Sector — Network optimization, Customer service Chatbots, Predictive maintenance, 5G, IoT, and Edge computing. Expected Growth in AI and Data Science Jobs: Job Market Growth: Expected CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate) of 30–40%. By 2030, India could see hundreds of thousands of AI and data science job openings in Tech companies, Startups, and Industries adopting data-driven transformation. Salary Trends: Entry-level roles (0–2 years experience): Rs. 6 – Rs. 12 Lakhs per annum Mid-level roles (3–5 years experience): Rs. 12 – Rs. 25 Lakhs per annum Senior-level roles (5+ years experience): Rs. 25 – Rs. 50 Lakhs per annum AI Research Scientists: Rs. 30 – Rs. 70 Lakhs per annum Skills and Certifications Required for AI and Data Science Jobs: Technical Skills: 1. Python, R, and SQL for data manipulation and machine learning algorithms. 2. Machine learning frameworks: TensorFlow, Keras, and PyTorch. 3. Cloud platforms (AWS, Google Cloud, Azure) for deploying AI models. 4. Big data tools such as Hadoop, Spark, and Kafka. 5. Advanced certifications from platforms like Coursera, edX, and Udacity. Recommended Certifications: 1. Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer 2. AWS Certified Machine Learning Specialty 3. Data Science Specialization by IBM

section: Department Overview & Programme Details — B.E. AI & ML
page_title: Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
text: Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Overview: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a technology that makes machines reproduce human behavior. Machine Learning (ML) is a sub-part of AI by which machines automatically learn from past data without programming. AI and ML together innovate human-like intelligent computer systems to solve complex problems. B.Tech in CSE with Specialization in AIML (CSE-AIML) provides budding engineers with courses dedicated to frontiers in AI and ML with a foundation of Computer Science & Engineering. This course enhances knowledge of computer technologies including programming, coding, database and web development. Engineers with AI expertise will be needed in crucial domains such as Healthcare, Industry 4.0, Finance, Agriculture, Security, Law, and Environment Management. The curriculum covers Computational Mathematics, core Computer Science, and latest advancements in AI and ML. Specialization areas include machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, computer vision, data mining, Explainable AI, Generative Adversarial Networks, Multimodal AI and Regenerative AI. Tools and technologies: Python, R, TensorFlow, Spark, Hadoop. Career Prospects: Big Data Engineer, Business Intelligence Developer, Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, Research Scientist, AI Data Analyst, AI Engineer, Robotics Scientist. Recruiting companies include Microsoft, Amazon, Goldman Sachs, Oracle GBU, Cisco, Dell Technologies, Accenture. Programme Details — B.E. Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning: Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry). No. of Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry). Total Seats: 60. Government Quota: 30. Management Quota: 30. Eligibility: 10+2 with pass in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. Curriculum: 2021 Regulation — https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf. Scope for Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.

section: Vision, Mission, PEOs, PSOs & Programme Outcomes (POs)
page_title: Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
text: Vision of AIML Department: To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Engineers using through sustainable practices. Mission of AIML Department: M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science through innovative teaching and learning methodology. M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society. M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability. Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs): PEO1. Graduates will be prepared for analysing, designing, developing and testing the software solutions and products with creativity and sustainability. PEO2. Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements. PEO3. Graduates will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms. Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs): PSO1. Develop creative solutions by adapting emerging technologies/tools for real time applications of Industry. PSO2. Apply the acquired knowledge to develop software solutions and innovative mobile apps for various automation applications. Programme Outcomes (POs): PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions. PO3 Design/Development of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet specified needs. PO4 Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data. PO5 Modern

section: AI & ML Job Roles, Salary Trends & Employment Forecast India 2025-2030
page_title: Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
text: AI and ML Employment Forecast in India 2025-2030: The AI and ML sectors in India are poised for substantial growth over the next five years. Key Drivers: 1. Increased AI Adoption Across Industries — AI/ML adopted across healthcare, finance, e-commerce, retail, manufacturing, agriculture. Companies leveraging AI/ML for automation, data analytics, predictive analytics, personalization, customer service. Indian companies like Infosys, Wipro, TCS investing heavily in AI/ML. 2. Government Initiatives — Digital India, National AI Strategy, AI for All, AI-enabled Smart Cities, AI-powered education. 3. AI and Automation in Enterprises — Robotic Process Automation (RPA) integrated with AI and NLP creating job opportunities. 4. R&D and Innovation — Google, Microsoft, IBM, Intel have set up R&D centers in India. 5. Increased Investment in Startups — AI startups attracting substantial venture capital investment. 6. Healthcare, Autonomous Vehicles, Smart Cities — Major AI integration areas. Key AI and ML Job Roles (2025-2030): Machine Learning Engineer — Develop and deploy ML models. Skills: Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, neural networks, deep learning. High demand in e-commerce, finance, automotive, manufacturing. Data Scientist — Analyze and interpret complex data. Skills: Python, R, SQL, Matplotlib, Seaborn, ML algorithms, big data tools. In demand in finance, retail, healthcare, telecom. AI Research Scientist — Conduct cutting-edge research in NLP, computer vision, reinforcement learning. Skills: Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, mathematical modeling. Deep Learning Engineer — Build neural networks for computer vision, NLP, speech recognition. Skills: TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch. NLP Engineer — Develop AI systems for human language using ML techniques. Skills: SpaCy, NLTK, sentiment analysis, speech recognition. AI Software Engineer — Design and build AI applications. Skills: Python, C++, Java, TensorFlow, cloud computing, Docker, Kubernetes. AI Ethics Specialist — Ens

section: Industry-Specific AI Trends, Certifications & Targeted Job Applications
page_title: Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
text: Industry-Specific AI and ML Job Trends: Healthcare — Medical diagnostics, Personalized treatment, Drug discovery, Robot-assisted surgery, Medical AI, Bioinformatics, and healthcare data analytics. Finance and Banking — Fraud detection, Credit scoring, Personalized banking services, Automated trading, Financial data scientists, Quantitative analysts, AI-enabled risk management. ML applications in Fintech will create opportunities for specialists in Blockchain, smart contracts, and AI in Cryptocurrency. Automotive (Autonomous Vehicles) — Self-driving cars and Smart transportation systems. AI and ML professionals required for computer vision, sensor fusion, and real-time decision-making for autonomous vehicles. Retail and E-commerce — AI transforming customer experience through personalized recommendations, dynamic pricing. Demand for professionals in e-commerce AI, recommendation algorithms, and supply chain optimization. Certifications and Skills to Upskill: 1. Python and R language for data manipulation and model development. 2. TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Keras for deep learning. 3. Data science tools: Pandas, NumPy, SciPy for data wrangling and analysis. 4. NLP frameworks: SpaCy, Hugging Face for text processing and sentiment analysis. 5. Google AI Certification and Deep Learning Specialization by Andrew Ng (Coursera). 6. Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer. 7. AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty. 8. Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer Associate. 9. IBM AI Engineering Professional Certificate. 10. Data Science Professional Certificate. 11. Certified Data Scientist (CDS). 12. Deep Learning Specialization by Andrew Ng. 13. Advanced Machine Learning Specialization. 14. Natural Language Processing with Deep Learning. Targeted Job Applications: AI-centric companies — Google, Microsoft, Amazon, NVIDIA, IBM, TCS, and Infosys. Startups — India's growing AI/ML startups in Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Pune offer hands-on work in cutting-edge AI/ML applications
